activities_projects.title: Measuring Time with Stopwatch
steps: Select a simple activity like running 10 meters
Use stopwatch to measure time taken
Record the time in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands use of stopwatch for time measurement

activities_projects.title: Comparing Time Measurement Devices
activities_projects.objective: Observe differences between clocks and stopwatches
steps: Measure the time for the same activity using both devices
Compare readings
Discuss which device is more precise
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands precision of different time-measuring devices

activities_projects.title: Measuring Speed of a Walking Person
steps: Measure a known distance
Time a person walking this distance
Calculate speed using formula speed = distance/time
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies basic formula to calculate speed

activities_projects.title: Observing Motion with a Toy Car
steps: Mark distances along a straight path
Move toy car and record time at each mark
Calculate speed between each segment and observe if constant

activities_projects.title: Time Measurement of Pendulum Swing
activities_projects.objective: Measure periodic motion using a pendulum
steps: Suspend the weight to make a simple pendulum
Displace slightly and release
Measure time for 10 swings using stopwatch
Calculate time period for one swing
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands periodic motion and time period
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurement of swings and time period (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Graphing Distance vs Time
steps: Record distance and corresponding time for an activity
Plot a distance-time graph
Analyze slope to determine speed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns how to represent motion graphically
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct graph plotting and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reaction Time Measurement
steps: Hold a ruler vertically between thumb and finger
Partner releases ruler without warning
Catch it as quickly as possible and record distance fallen
Calculate reaction time using formula t = √(2s/g)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Measures reaction time and relates it to motion formula

activities_projects.title: Speed Comparison of Two Vehicles
activities_projects.objective: Compare speeds using distance and time
steps: Mark same distance for both vehicles
Time each vehicle moving the distance
Calculate and compare speeds

activities_projects.title: Effect of Slope on Motion
steps: Set up a ramp at different angles
Time toy car rolling down ramp
Record speed for each angle and analyze

activities_projects.title: Create Your Own Motion Quiz
steps: Prepare five questions on time measurement
Prepare five questions on motion and speed
Exchange with a friend and solve
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Strengthens overall understanding of motion and time
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of questions and answers (10 marks)
